title: Basic Parts - A Flan's Mod Content Pack
description: An essential pack for the discerning crafter of Flan's Mod tech. Gain access to a wide variety of parts, from barrels and stocks to engines and armour plating.
body:
![A row of icons from Basic Parts](https://i.imgur.com/5jcOLvY.png)

# Basic Parts - A Flan's Mod Content Pack

![A row of icons from Basic Parts](https://i.imgur.com/D0ZoTPO.png)
![A row of icons from Basic Parts](https://i.imgur.com/yYlaeEu.png)

An essential pack for the discerning crafter of Flan's Mod tech. Gain access to a wide variety of parts, from barrels and stocks to engines and armour plating.
Each part type has between 3 and 10 options, each made of a different material.

There are also a wide range of munitions, varying in size (pistol rounds to artillery shells) and in effects (armour piercing, explosive or incendiary for example).


## Crafting Stations
The **Bullet Fabricator** and **Part Fabricator** are your key to making most things in this pack. Place them down and click to see a full list of available recipes

![Crafting recipes for the workbenches](https://i.imgur.com/LZJu7aN.png)

## The Fabrication Interface

The fabricator menus have a lot of sections, so let's dig in. 

- **Input** - Place items from your inventory here. For "materials", that is to say anything that has a block, ingot and nugget form or similar, you can input any type and the fabricator will automatically manage the items and split as necessary
- **Part Fabrication** - The main central panel lists all the recipes available in this fabricator (the Part Fabricator and Bullet Fabricator have different lists!). You can use the tickboxes on the filter bar to only see recipes you can craft, or to restrict to certain tiers of outputs. When you select an item from this list, further details will be displayed in the blueprint area and a summary of how many you can craft will be shown.
- **Craft Buttons** - Once you have selected a recipe, click to send a craft action to the queue.
- **Queue** - All the parts you have requested will enter the crafting queue and be processed over time. You can cancel (all but the currently in-progress item) by pressing the X
- **Output** - Completed crafted items will be placed here. Shift-click them to get them into your inventory or add an item transport system to the fabricator.

![The Part Fabrication screen](https://i.imgur.com/2m7IEbO.png)

## Distillation

But how do you get Fiberglass, Polyester, PhanTex and more? That's where distillation comes in. To distill raw materials into advanced polymers, you will need to create a Distillation Cap and at least one Distillation Stack. Some distillation recipes will only need one layer, but some will require up to 3 layers to get every possible output.

_Warning: If you run a distillation that has 3 outputs, but only provide one stack below the cap, you will miss out on the deeper fractional outputs. Fortunately, the distillation menu will let you know that this is the case_

![Distillation cap recipe](https://i.imgur.com/oqyLQUG.png)
![Distillation Tower Recipe](https://i.imgur.com/ez50bJ2.png)

To assemble the multiblock structure, simply place any number of Distillation Stacks and put a Distillation Cap on top.

![A Distillation Tower and some blocks of materials](https://i.imgur.com/sg1Kz7i.png)

## Distillation Recipes

![Distilling Carbon Fiber](https://i.imgur.com/quvKwh7.png)
![Distilling PhanTex](https://i.imgur.com/oseQAuF.png)
![Distilling Polyester](https://i.imgur.com/HSQZcH5.png)
![Distilling Kevlar](https://i.imgur.com/VaCrHtC.png)

## Other Materials

You can find two new metals by blasting Iron and Netherite

![Blasting Iron into Steel](https://i.imgur.com/sqqBl4V.png)
![Blasting Netherite into Nethersteel](https://i.imgur.com/m1XSkxX.png)

The precursor for distilling Fiberglass and Kevlar is Silicate, a mix of Sand and Clay

![Mixing Silicate for Polymer](https://i.imgur.com/plWh0V3.png)


Wood is categorized into 3 broad types, one per tier. You can input logs or planks of the corresponding type

- **Soft Wood** is Oak, Acacia, Birch and Dark Oak
- **Hard Wood** is Cherry, Mangrove and Jungle
- **Dense Wood** is Warped and Crimson

![Wood types in the Part Fabricator](https://i.imgur.com/0Ze2vfW.png)



[Coming Soon]
Materials will provide stat modifiers and abilities to the weapons and vehicles you create
